title: viscum album subsp. abietis
intro: Viscum album subsp. abietis, also known as European mistletoe, is a semi-parasitic plant that grows on the branches of various tree species, such as oak and ash. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing stress, improving immune function, and supporting cardiovascular health. Viscum album subsp. abietis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including extracts, tinctures, teas, and infusions, which are often used in traditional medicine and complementary therapies.
